displayTitle: Where the Test Becomes a Decision
kicker: ET LEVEL I // READOUTS & THE IMPEDANCE PLANE

beats.narration: Every eddy current test ends at a readout, where an impedance change becomes a decision. The coil can be perfect; a failed display fails the whole test.

payload.title: THE READOUT
payload.sub: Where an impedance change becomes a decision.

beats.narration: A meter collapses the signal to one number. Calibrated, it reads engineering units like percent IACS. Uncalibrated, just arbitrary divisions — a go, no-go comparator.

payload.heading: A meter gives you one number

beats.narration: The impedance plane adds a second dimension. The dot's direction, its phase angle, names the variable; its distance, the amplitude, says how much.

payload.heading: Direction names it, distance sizes it
payload.note: PHASE = WHICH VARIABLE · AMPLITUDE = HOW MUCH

beats.narration: Rotate lift-off horizontal during setup. Now probe wobble slides flat while a crack breaks away at its own angle, into the alarm gate you positioned.

chips.s: A distinct phase angle stands out

beats.narration: Analog drew a flying dot on a tube. Digital samples it and adds freeze, storage, and gates. So respect the scan speed in your instruction.

payload.sub: Scan too fast and sampling can miss the gate.
